title: Intensely Tangy Egyptian Shrimp Tagine
description: A tangy and juicy Egyptian dish featuring jumbo shrimp, perfect for a post-workout meal, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot using a pressure cooker for a kosher friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lemon juice and bring the mixture to a boil.
Add the jumbo shrimp and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cover the tandoor clay pot and transfer it to a pressure cooker.
Cook the shrimp in the pressure cooker for 5 minutes, or until they are pink and fully cooked.
Remove the tandoor clay pot from the pressure cooker and garnish the shrimp with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the shrimp hot, with crusty bread or over rice.
